feat: AI-proof editing — ast.parse() gate, rollback, structured errors

Pre-write validation: _validate_python_syntax() runs ast.parse() on new
content before _write_and_cache() writes to disk. Broken Python never
reaches the filesystem.

Structured error output: _parse_error_for_agent() converts SyntaxError
into actionable instructions with line numbers, context window, and
caret markers — the LLM can self-correct without re-reading the file.

Rollback on failure: _rollback_edit() restores .bak backup and
invalidates _step_file_cache when validation fails. No half-written
files left behind.

Create file guard: _act_create_file() validates syntax before writing
new .py files, preventing creation of files with broken syntax.

pixel-count guardrails, move heavy image processing to a Web Worker

rendering (fast preview + debounced full-res settle).

available space (w-full h-full) instead of wrapping tightly around the image.
The image is centered within the widget. Outer padding (p-3) provides breathing
room from viewport edges for floating panels. Both the "no image" and "image
loaded" states share the same widget container with containerRef always attached
for proper ResizeObserver operation.

commit.message:
Redesign app UI with modern Material 3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add dashboard UI with real-time active song syncing via useSyncedState (#12)

Adds a TV-friendly dashboard page (`/:token/dashboard`) for controlling
which song is currently playing, with real-time sync to all connected
vote pages via rwsdk's `useSyncedState`.

### Infrastructure
- Added `SyncedStateServer` Durable Object binding and v2 migration in
`wrangler.jsonc`
- Registered `syncedStateRoutes` and exported `SyncedStateServer` from
`worker.tsx`

### Dashboard (`/:token/dashboard`)
- Server component loads songs; client component (`DashboardControls`)
renders play/pause buttons per song
- State scoped to game ID via third parameter: `useSyncedState<string |
null>(null, "activeSong", gameId)`
- "Now playing" banner always reserves vertical space — shows dimmed "No
song currently playing" placeholder when idle, preventing layout shifts
- Accessible `aria-label`/`aria-pressed` on all buttons

### Vote page active song highlight
- `useActiveSong(gameId)` custom hook subscribes to the synced state key
- `VoteSongList` client component uses the hook and conditionally
applies Tailwind CSS `before:` pseudo-element classes
(`before:bg-linear-to-br before:from-indigo-500/35
before:via-purple-500/25 before:to-indigo-500/15`) for a translucent
indigo→purple gradient ring on the active song
- Custom pulsing animation defined in `styles.css` as a CSS custom
property (`--animate-active-song-pulse`), applied via
`animate-(--animate-active-song-pulse)`
- `aria-live="polite"` region announces the currently playing song for
assistive tech

### Screenshots

**Dashboard (no song playing)**
![Dashboard no
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79f2d7c4-9f2a-4d38-9d00-d2c7a3e3c6ea)

**Vote page (active song highlighted)**
![Vote active
song](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/975b3e0b-d1d5-460f-8fb9-4aefda37219b)

### E2E tests (3 new, 19 total passing)
- Dashboard rendering and song selection/deselection
- Cross-page real-time sync: two browser pages open simultaneously,
clicking a song on the dashboard asserts that
`[data-testid="now-playing"]` updates on the vote page without refresh

```tsx
// Dashboard sets the active song scoped to this game
const [activeSong, setActiveSong] = useSyncedState<string | null>(null, "activeSong", gameId);

// Vote page subscribes via custom hook — updates arrive via WebSocket
const activeSong = useActiveSong(gameId);
```

---------

commit.message:
fix: P95 domain adversarial patient_idx indexing bug

batch.patient_idx is per-graph [B] from PyG batching (scalar attrs are
stacked), NOT per-node [N]. Code was indexing with batch.ptr[gi] (a node
offset) which exceeds tensor size for any graph with >1 node.

Fix: use batch.patient_idx directly + store as torch.tensor for explicitness.

* Fix noisy audio: rebuild Windows DLL with libvgmstream_setup PCM16 output

Previous commit bb7ea19 fixed Linux .so but missed the Windows .dll.
The user was running the old Windows DLL which output float32 (4 bytes/sample),
causing noise when Godot's AudioStreamWAV FORMAT_16_BITS tried to interpret
float bytes as int16.

Both binaries now rebuilt with libvgmstream_setup(force_sfmt=PCM16):
- libgodot_rocksmith.so: 5,376,000 bytes decoded (PCM16 confirmed, was 10,752,000 float32)
- godot_rocksmith.dll: rebuilt with same fix (was never updated after bb7ea19)

Verified on Linux sandbox:
  AudioEngine: decoded 496786 WEM bytes → 5376000 PCM bytes (2 ch, 48000 Hz)
  = 28s × 48000 × 2ch × 2 bytes (PCM16) ✓

Force fresh build by updating Dockerfile comment

fix: update duration from stream_details, clear stale duration on advance

Duration was not updating on track auto-advance because _advance_queue
sent the old track's duration_ms to Ynison, which echoed it back.

Three fixes:
- _advance_queue / _on_previous: set duration_ms=0 to prevent
  propagating stale duration from the previous track
- _update_metadata_from_stream: new method called from _stream_track
  that updates duration and elapsed_time from stream_details (the
  authoritative source) with force_update=True
- Import StreamDetails for the type annotation

commit.message:
fix: downgrade REQUEST_CHANGES to COMMENT on bot's own PRs (#51)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 — GitHub doesn't allow `REQUEST_CHANGES`
on your own PR. The bot created #49 via `/implement`, then tried to
review it with `REQUEST_CHANGES`.

## Fix

When reviewing a PR authored by the bot, downgrade `REQUEST_CHANGES` to
`COMMENT`. The feedback still gets posted, it just doesn't block.

---------
